name: West Point (USMA)
tagline: The United States Military Academy on the Hudson River, the Army's oldest post and its officer-producing institution.
_note: United States Military Academy + USAG West Point (Hudson Valley, NY). CONUS full template (BAH MHA NY217 West Point; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). Population is cadets plus faculty/staff. On-post schools operated by DoDEA Americas (West Point Elementary & Middle); HS via Highland Falls. Medical: Keller Army Community Hospital. Facts verified from official sources (westpoint.edu, home.army.mil/westpoint, Keller ACH, DeCA/AAFES, DoDEA Americas, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). New York is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 10996 (West Point) / Highland Falls.

data.Location: West Point, NY (Hudson Valley, ~50 mi north of NYC)
data.Role: The United States Military Academy (USMA), the Army's four-year officer academy
data.Founded: 1802; the Army's oldest continuously operating post
data.Population: Cadets plus assigned faculty, staff, and their families
data.Setting: Historic campus on the Hudson River

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: West Point is the United States Military Academy, the Army's four-year college that educates and commissions new officers, and it is the Army's oldest continuously operating post. The cadet population defines the place, supported by a large faculty and staff.
It sits on the Hudson River about 50 miles north of New York City, a historic, scenic campus where assignments are typically as faculty, staff, or in the garrison rather than an operational unit.

sections.title: A Faculty/Staff Tour in the Hudson Valley

data.subtitle: West Point is a staff-and-faculty posting, not an operational one. The main choice is on-post housing for the short commute versus the Hudson Valley towns, with New York City within reach.
intro: Most families here are assigned as faculty, staff, or garrison, and many take on-post housing given the historic, somewhat enclosed campus. Off post means the Hudson Valley, which is scenic but can be pricey closer to the city.
Where you live is a balance of commute, schools, and how much Hudson Valley (or NYC) access you want, since the metro area is roughly an hour-plus south.

recommendations.advice: On-post housing keeps you minutes from work and the academy's schools and services; it's a popular choice here.
Highland Falls sits right outside the gate; Cornwall, New Windsor, and the Newburgh area offer more options a short drive out.
Heading north and west in the Hudson Valley generally trades a longer commute for lower costs.
New York City is about an hour-plus south by car or train, great for visits but a long daily commute.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: West Point is a controlled-access historic post. Visitors without a DoD ID need a pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID, typically processed through the Visitors Control Center; tourism to the academy is also coordinated through the visitor center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official West Point site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: West Point has on-post family housing across historic and newer neighborhoods, popular with faculty and staff for the short commute.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA NY217 (West Point)

sections.title: Schools
data: West Point has on-post schools operated by DoDEA Americas (West Point Elementary and West Point Middle School). High-school students typically attend James I. O'Neill High School in the Highland Falls-Fort Montgomery district just outside the gate.
Other Hudson Valley districts serve families who live farther out, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Primary and specialty outpatient care with some inpatient services on post; complex care referred to the regional network

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and at West Point many staff and faculty take on-post housing rather than buy in the Hudson Valley.
rent: Your tour is a standard length (often three years for faculty) and you'll PCS out.
You prefer on-post housing for the short commute and community.
You'd rather not navigate a varied Hudson Valley market on a fixed timeline.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect a longer tour or repeat assignments in the area.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
You've found a Hudson Valley town and commute you're confident about.
You're comfortable owning and possibly renting it out later.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, and many West Point families simply take on-post housing. If you buy, weigh Hudson Valley prices and your tour length. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book the on-post Five Star Inn or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: On-post housing is popular here; get on the list early and get the office's read on the Hudson Valley before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and New York vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact DoDEA Americas (on-post schools) or the Highland Falls district for high school early, and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Keller Army Community Hospital or network providers.

data.q: What kind of assignment is West Point?
data.a: It's the United States Military Academy. Tours here are typically as faculty, staff, or garrison supporting cadet education, not operational units.
Many take on-post housing for the short commute. Off post, Highland Falls is right outside the gate, with Cornwall, Newburgh, and the wider Hudson Valley farther out.
On-post elementary and middle schools are run by DoDEA Americas; high-school students generally attend O'Neill High School in Highland Falls.
West Point is MHA NY217. Use the calculator above for your grade.
